evidence: All 75 milestone_record tests pass (0 failures, 0 ignored)
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes cleanly (no warnings)

model.rs: TaskRunEntry has all required fields — milestone_id (275), bead_id (278), project_id (280), run_id (282), plan_hash (285), outcome (288), outcome_detail (290), started_at (293), finished_at (296) — all new optional fields use serde(default, skip_serializing_if) for backward compatibility

model.rs: Centralized attempt-matching helpers — same_attempt (301), merge_attempt_entries (312), collapse_task_run_attempts (399), active_bead_ids (336), find_matching_running_task_run (344), has_finalized_task_run (385) — shared by service and FS adapter
model.rs: validate_semantics (173-184) rejects Active without active_bead and terminal with active_bead

service.rs: reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage (212-268) now derives idle status from lineage — lines 261-265 use `!snapshot.status.is_terminal() && (has_any_task_runs || snapshot.status == MilestoneStatus::Active)` to transition stale non-terminal snapshots to Ready when lineage shows execution happened but no bead is active. This fixes amendment fr-5-15b1f41e.

service.rs: record_bead_start (451-496) delegates lineage start to locked `lineage_store.record_task_run_start()` (470) and journal dedupe to locked `journal_store.append_event_if_missing()` (493), addressing the TOCTOU race from amendment fr-5-ef6f1e4a

fs.rs: FsTaskRunLineageStore::record_task_run_start (2526-2603) acquires AdvisoryFileLock at line 2537 BEFORE reading task runs at line 2538, making the entire read-check-append-or-reuse sequence atomic under a single lock

fs.rs: FsMilestoneJournalStore::append_event_if_missing (2306-2327) acquires AdvisoryFileLock at line 2313 BEFORE reading journal and checking for duplicates, making journal dedupe atomic under a single lock

fs.rs: record_task_run_start correctly handles: other-active-bead rejection (2542-2551), already-finalized rejection (2553-2561), existing running entry reuse with optional backfill (2563-2581), new entry creation (2589-2602)

Test: completion_repair_moves_stale_planning_snapshot_to_ready (1645-1710) verifies the fr-5-15b1f41e fix: directly appends Running lineage row without updating snapshot (simulating crash after lineage write), then completes the bead; snapshot transitions from Planning to Ready with correct counters

Test: concurrent_start_retries_share_one_lineage_row_and_start_event (2072-2150) verifies the fr-5-ef6f1e4a fix: two threads simultaneously call record_bead_start with identical parameters using a Barrier; asserts exactly 1 lineage row and 1 BeadStarted journal event
Test: duplicate_running_rows_with_same_run_id_can_be_finalized (2153+) verifies that duplicate running rows (remnants of a race that bypassed locking) can still be finalized cleanly

service.rs: update_task_run (571-625) validates terminal outcome (585), updates lineage (593), reconciles snapshot from canonical lineage (606-610), validates semantics (613), writes snapshot (614), and deduplicates journal events (616-622)

service.rs: TaskRunLineagePort trait default implementation (96-154) provides unlocked version for in-memory test implementations; FsTaskRunLineageStore overrides with locked version for production use
findings_or_gaps: The trait default for record_task_run_start (service.rs:96-154) is unlocked; only the FS adapter override provides locking. This is acceptable because the trait default serves in-memory test implementations and the FS adapter is the sole production implementor, but if a new adapter is added it must also provide its own locking.

The snapshot read-write in record_bead_start (service.rs:463-487) is not under any lock — only lineage and journal writes are locked. Since reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage deterministically rebuilds state from canonical lineage, two concurrent writers produce the same correct snapshot, making the unlocked snapshot write benign for correctness. However, if snapshot writes are ever non-idempotent (e.g., counters that increment rather than rebuild), this would need revisiting.

AppError::RunStartFailed is still used (service.rs:586) for rejecting non-terminal outcomes in update_task_run, which is semantically a finalization rejection. A more specific variant would be cleaner but this is cosmetic, not a correctness issue — carried forward from prior reviews.

The outstanding risk noted by the implementor — that start/completion still spans lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction — remains. All three paths are now replay-safe (lineage locks + journal locks + deterministic snapshot reconciliation), making this an acceptable trade-off for file-based storage.
